title: leontodon incanus subsp. tenuiflorus
intro: Leontodon incanus subsp. tenuiflorus, also known as the lesser hawk's beard, is a plant species in the dandelion family with a distinctive white or pale yellow flower and a hairy, grayish-green leaf. It has been used in traditional medicine for its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as urinary problems, skin conditions, and infections. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Leontodon incanus subsp. tenuiflorus can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
